seo.title: Nuxt implementation checklist
seo.description: A practical checklist for shipping Nuxt projects safely.

content.intro: This checklist ensures your Nuxt application is optimized for performance, security, and scalability before deployment to production environments such as Vercel, Netlify, or self-hosted Docker containers.

items.title: Enable Payload Extraction
items.description: Verify that 'experimental.payloadExtraction' is set to true in nuxt.config.ts to reduce the size of the initial HTML response.

items.title: Implement Nuxt Image
items.description: Replace standard tags with to enable automatic resizing, WebP conversion, and lazy loading.

items.title: Analyze Bundle Sizes
items.description: Run 'npx nuxi analyze' to identify large third-party dependencies and replace them with smaller alternatives or move them to server-only modules.

items.title: Configure Route Rules
items.description: Define SWR (Stale-While-Revalidate) or ISR (Incremental Static Regeneration) rules in nuxt.config.ts for high-traffic dynamic pages.

items.title: Optimize Font Loading
items.description: Use @nuxtjs/google-fonts with 'download: true' to host fonts locally and prevent layout shifts (CLS).

items.title: Validate Environment Variables
items.description: Use a validation schema (e.g., Zod) within a nitro plugin to ensure all NUXT_PUBLIC_* and NUXT_PRIVATE_* variables are present at runtime.

items.title: Install Nuxt Security Module
items.description: Configure 'nuxt-security' to automatically apply CSP headers, CORS policies, and Rate Limiting to server routes.

items.title: Sanitize Server Routes
items.description: Verify that all /server/api routes use readBody() or getQuery() with input validation to prevent injection attacks.

items.title: Disable Production DevTools
items.description: Ensure 'devtools: { enabled: false }' is explicitly set or conditionally disabled for production builds.

items.title: Restrict Public Assets
items.description: Audit the /public folder to ensure no sensitive configuration files, .env backups, or private keys are exposed.

items.title: Unique useFetch Keys
items.description: Ensure every manually keyed useFetch or useAsyncData call has a unique string key to prevent hydration mismatches during SSR.

items.title: Pinia Server-Side State
items.description: Verify that Pinia stores are initialized within the setup function to prevent state cross-contamination between different user requests.

items.title: Handle Fetch Errors
items.description: Implement 'fatal: true' in createError calls within fetch blocks to force a redirect to the error page when critical data fails.

items.title: Lazy Loading Data
items.description: Use useLazyFetch for non-critical UI components to prevent blocking the initial page navigation.

items.title: Cache API Responses
items.description: Use Nitro's 'useStorage' or 'defineCachedEventHandler' to cache expensive database queries or external API calls.

items.title: Dynamic Meta Tags
items.description: Use useSeoMeta() in every page component to define unique titles, descriptions, and Open Graph images.

items.title: Sitemap Generation
items.description: Configure @nuxtjs/sitemap to automatically crawl routes and include dynamic slugs from your CMS or database.

items.title: Robots.txt Configuration
items.description: Add @nuxtjs/robots to prevent indexing of staging environments and private admin routes.

items.title: Canonical URL Implementation
items.description: Verify that useHead() includes a canonical link tag to prevent duplicate content penalties from search engines.

items.title: Structured Data
items.description: Inject JSON-LD scripts using useHead for product, article, or organization schemas to improve rich snippet visibility.

items.title: Global Error Page
items.description: Create a custom error.vue in the root directory to handle 404 and 500 errors with a user-friendly UI.

items.title: Sentry Integration
items.description: Configure Sentry or a similar observability tool to capture both client-side Vue errors and server-side Nitro errors.

items.title: Hydration Mismatch Audit
items.description: Run the build in a staging environment and check the browser console for hydration mismatches that could break interactivity.

items.title: Structured Server Logs
items.description: Ensure Nitro server logs are outputting in JSON format for easier ingestion by log management platforms like Datadog or Logtail.

items.title: Route Middleware Guards
items.description: Verify that auth middleware correctly redirects unauthenticated users before the page component begins mounting.

items.title: Nitro Preset Selection
items.description: Explicitly set the 'NITRO_PRESET' environment variable (e.g., vercel, netlify, or node-server) to match your hosting provider.

items.title: Prisma/Drizzle Migration Check
items.description: Include a database migration step in your CI/CD pipeline before the 'nuxt build' command executes.

items.title: Health Check Endpoint
items.description: Create a /server/api/health.ts route that returns a 200 status to allow load balancers to monitor instance readiness.

items.title: Brotli Compression
items.description: Ensure the hosting provider or reverse proxy (Nginx) is configured to serve .mjs and .css files with Brotli compression.

items.title: Source Map Generation
items.description: Disable production source maps in nuxt.config.ts unless they are specifically required for your error reporting service.
